messages.content:
Clear-cutting ________.

is a form of violent protest favoured by radical environmental organizations

is harvesting trees from coastal areas only

restricts timber harvesting to mountaintops

removes all trees from an area

involves the elimination of subsidies to farmers
removes all trees from an area

What has become the most effective way to achieve compliance for conservation planning?
None of the above

Jailing those who violate or bend the rules

All of the above

Gathering all interested parties to discuss and decide on a course of action

Imposing hefty fines on infractors
Gathering all interested parties to discuss and decide on a course of action.

What was the origin conservation problem discussed in the book “Silent Spring” written by Rachel Carson in 1962?

The impact of wide-spread used chemicals on agriculture

The impact of illegal cutting

The impact of industrial pollution on water resources

The impact of pesticides on animals
The impact of pesticides on animals.

Habitat fragmentation can increase both biodiversity and ecological complexity in an area.

True

False
False. Habitat fragmentation typically decreases biodiversity and ecological complexity in an area.

Biodiversity conservation may help improve food security because ________.

it decreases the number of pollinators that may help crops reproduce

it increases the genetic diversity that may benefit crops

it decreases the number of potential predators that may feed on major crops

None is correct.

it decreases the number of available pathogens that may weaken crops
it increases the genetic diversity that may benefit crops.

Considering the data input of GIS, fill in the following blanks.

The system of earth-orbiting satellites which can provide the precise location of an object is called 
 .

The use of aircraft or satellites to capture information about Earth's surface is called 
 .

The data collected manually via people is the method of 
 .

The system of earth-orbiting satellites which can provide the precise location of an object is called GPS (Global Positioning System).

The use of aircraft or satellites to capture information about Earth's surface is called remote sensing.

The data collected manually via people is the method of fieldwork.

Which of the following statement is true?

None is correct.

All GIS models are wrong due to their simple representations of the reality.

No GIS model can help the studies of genetic diversity.

GIS cannot determine the operability of a forest stand due to its complexity.

The suitability of a habitat can be informed by the 3D driver simulation.

Hydrological analysis need the FORCAST model to evaluate streams and buffers.

Both harvest modelling and yield predictions rely on the model of SIMFOR.
The suitability of a habitat can be informed by the 3D driver simulation.

Which of the following statement is NOT true?

GIS needs to incorporate databases with spatial information in an integrated system.

Only a few GIS projects do not need a large amount of time for data collection and database development.

GIS is a computer-based system that involves people and data.

GIS is about formatting and analyzing the geographic and geospatial information in map forms.

All options are true.

GIS can tell us about what the place has and where the place is, but not when the place was studied
Only a few GIS projects do not need a large amount of time for data collection and database development.

Which of the following is wrong?

Polygons do not belong to raster datasets.

The raster structure of GIS allows data to be stored in grids.

A grid refers to the intersection of a row and a column in raster data.

The cell of a raster dataset can always contain latitudinal, longitudinal, and elevation information.

All options are correct.

GIS is organized in layers of raster and vector data.

The cell of a raster dataset can always contain latitudinal, longitudinal, and elevation information. The cell of a raster dataset typically contains a single value representing an attribute or a measurement at a particular location, such as elevation or temperature. It may or may not contain latitudinal and longitudinal information depending on the structure of the raster dataset.

To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, which condition needs to be achieved?

1.Populations are large
2.Random mating among nearby individual
3.No mutation, selection, and migration


1

2

2 & 3

1 & 3

3

1, 2 & 3. To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, populations should be large, mating among individuals should be random and there should be no mutation, natural selection or migration into or out of the population.

To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, which condition needs to be achieved?

1.Populations are large
2.Random mating among nearby individual
3.No mutation, selection, and migration
1

2

2 & 3

1 & 3

3

1, 2 & 3. To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, populations should be large, mating among individuals should be random and there should be no mutation, natural selection or migration into or out of the population.

To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, which condition needs to be achieved?

Populations are large
Random mating among nearby individual
No mutation, selection, and migration

To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, populations should be large, mating among individuals should be random and there should be no mutation, natural selection or migration into or out of the population.
选哪个
I'm sorry, I can't answer this question without more context or options to choose from. Please provide more information.

To make gene pool frequencies remain unchanged, which condition needs to be achieved?

Populations are large
Random mating among nearby individual
No mutation, selection, and migration三个中选哪几个
需要同时满足三个条件，即1、2和3。只有当种群数量大、个体之间杂交随机、没有突变、天然选择或者个体迁入或迁出种群时，基因库频率才能保持不变。
